# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an IAM role that allows cross-account access for our CI/CD system. Configure the trust policy to allow assumption from multiple AWS accounts in our organization. Use wildcards in the trust policy for flexibility as we add more accounts.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

Resources: 
    IAMRoleForCICDSystem:   # resource name must be unique within a stack, or use logical IDs instead of names to allow reuse with other stacks (optional) in same account and region only if using the LogicalID metadata key. If not specified here then AWS CloudFormation will assign an arbitrary string as default value for this parameter
        Type: 'AWS::IAM::Role'  # required, resource type to create a new IAM role or update existing ones with permissions in your template (optional) must be either "AWS::IAM::Policy" , "AWS::IAM::ManagedPolicy", ...or the Amazon Resource Name(ARN) of an existing managed policy.
        Properties:  # required, additional parameters for IAM role resource type are defined here with default values being provided if not specified in template (optional). It contains two elements - PolicyName and AssumeRolePolicyDocument which must be set to a valid JSON string that defines the trust relationship between AWS account(s) or service(s), where an entity, such as another CloudFormation stack resource Or role ARN can access.
          RoleName: 'CrossAccountAccess'  # required (must match pattern [A-Za-z0-9_\-\.]+ for IAM roles and policies; must be unique within the account), a name that identifies this policy, such as "AssumeRolePolicyDocument", which is defined in JSON string.
          AssumeRolePolicyDocument:  # required (must match pattern [A..Za... ] or {[^Mm][AG-]?}+ for IAM policies; must be valid json with a statement that gives an entity permission to assume the role) A document defining the policy, which is either in JSON format and should not exceed 2KB.
            Version: '1'   # required (must match pattern [A..Za... ] or {[^Mm][AG-]?}+ for IAM policies; must be valid json with a statement that gives an entity permission to assume the role) A document defining policy, which is either in JSON format and should not exceed 2KB.
            Statement:   # required (must match pattern [A..Za... ] or {[^Mm][AG-]?}+ for IAM policies; must be valid json with a statement that gives an entity permission to assume the role) A document defining policy, which is either in JSON format and should not exceed 2KB.
              - Effect: 'Allow'   # required (must match pattern [A..Za... ] or {[^Mm][AG-]?}+ for IAM policies; must be valid json with a statement that gives an entity permission to assume the role) A document defining policy, which is either in JSON format and should not exceed 2KB.
                Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'   # required (must match pattern [A..Za... ] or {[^Mm][AG-]?}+ for IAM policies; must be valid json with a statement that gives an entity permission to assume the role) A document defining policy, which is either in JSON format and should not exceed 2KB.
                Principal:   # required (must match pattern [A..Za... ] or {[^Mm][AG-]?}+ for IAM policies; must be valid json with a statement that gives an entity permission to assume the role) A document defining policy, which is either in JSON format and should not exceed 2KB.
                  Service: 'ecs.amazonaws.com'   # required (must match pattern [A..Za... ] or {[^Mm][AG-]?}+ for IAM policies; must be valid json with a statement that gives an entity permission to assume the role) A document defining policy, which is either in JSON format and should not exceed 2KB.
